questions.question: Compare your shoulder and hip width:
How is your waistline?
options: My waist and hips are straight

shoulder-wide.name: Make Shoulders Look Wider
shoulder-wide.description: Use light-colored tops and boat neck/puff sleeves to emphasize the shoulder line.

shoulder-narrow.name: Make Shoulders Look Narrower
shoulder-narrow.description: Use dark-colored tops to visually reduce shoulders, and light bottoms to distribute attention.

belly.name: Minimize Belly Appearance
belly.description: Use dark-colored tops/mid layers to visually conceal the belly area.

legs-long.name: Make Legs Look Longer
legs-long.description: Match bottom and shoe colors to create a longer leg line.

height-tall.name: Look Taller
height-tall.description: Emphasize vertical lines with solid colors or similar tones.

none.name: Color Harmony Only
none.description: Focus purely on color harmony without body shape compensation.

female.desc: You have broad shoulders/chest and relatively slim lower body. Adding volume to the lower body and softening the upper body creates balance.
doList: Deep V-neck, narrow scoop neck, U-neck, or asymmetric neckline to narrow shoulders
Light colors and patterns on bottoms to add lower body volume
Keep tops simple with wrap or empire waist styles
A-line, pleated, tulip, or voluminous skirts for lower body emphasis
Wide-leg, bootcut, or boyfriend-fit pants for lower body volume
Define waistline with belts or wrap jackets
Raglan, kimono, or dolman sleeves to soften shoulder line
dontList: Off-shoulder, boat neck, square neck — makes shoulders look wider
Shoulder pads, puff sleeves, shoulder embellishments
Skinny jeans alone (makes lower body look thinner)
Horizontal stripes or large patterns on tops

male.desc: You have broad shoulders and a developed upper body. Adding volume to the lower body improves balance.
doList: V-neck to visually reduce shoulders and emphasize vertical lines
Light-colored bottoms to emphasize lower body volume
Dark solid colors on tops to visually reduce shoulders
Cargo pants or wide pants for lower body volume
Raglan sleeves to soften shoulder line
Single-breasted, slim lapel jackets
Straight-fit or bootcut pants
dontList: Boat neck — makes shoulders look wider

female.desc: Your lower body is wider than your upper body. Drawing attention to the upper body and streamlining the lower body improves overall proportions.
doList: Boat neck, off-shoulder, square neck to widen shoulder line
Light colors, patterns, and embellishments on tops to draw eyes up
Jackets and blazers with shoulder pads
A-line, tulip, full circle, bias-cut skirts
High-waist bootcut, wide-leg, or straight pants
Crop jackets, trench coats with waist tie
Wrap dresses, fit-and-flare dresses
dontList: Light colors, patterns, or embellishments on lower body

male.desc: Your lower body is wider than your upper body. Adding focal points to the upper body improves overall proportions.
doList: Structured blazers and shoulder-padded jackets to widen shoulders
Light colors and patterns on tops to draw eyes up
Chest details (pockets, epaulettes, horizontal stripes)
Dark solid colors on bottoms to streamline
Straight-fit, flat-front pants
V-neck or crew neck sweaters
Medium lapel single-breasted jackets

rectangle.name: Rectangle
rectangle.subtitle: Straight silhouette with similar shoulders, waist, and hips
rectangle.colorSummary: Create proportions with top-bottom color contrast

female.desc: You have a generally straight body shape. Creating a waistline or adding curves makes a great look.
doList: V-neck, scoop neck, sweetheart neck for vertical lines and curves
Wrap tops, peplum, belted blouses to define waist
Wrap dresses, fit-and-flare, belted dresses
A-line, wrap, asymmetric skirts
High-waist bootcut or wide-leg pants
Puff, flutter, balloon sleeves for volume
Texture mixing (knit + denim, etc.) layering
Belt to accentuate waistline (slim to medium width)
dontList: Boxy or loose fit alone (hides body lines even more)
Turtleneck or high neck (emphasizes straight lines)
Same-color top and bottom worn straight (looks flat)
Volume only at waist (breaks overall balance)

male.desc: You have a generally straight body shape. Creating dimension through layering and color contrast makes a great look.
doList: Create depth and dimension through layering
Accentuate waistline with belts or tuck-in
Divide proportions with top-bottom color contrast
Texture mixing (knit + denim, etc.)
Create vertical lines with jackets and cardigans
Crew neck t-shirts, V-neck sweaters
Structured blazers (with shoulder pads)
dontList: Same-color top and bottom worn straight (looks flat)
Boxy fit alone (hides body lines even more)
Long-line tops or coats (emphasizes straight vertical line)

hourglass.name: Hourglass
hourglass.subtitle: Balanced shoulders and hips with a defined waist
hourglass.colorSummary: Wear anything! Just accentuate the waist

female.desc: You have balanced shoulders and hips with a well-defined waist. Highlighting your natural lines looks amazing.
doList: Fits that accentuate the waistline (tuck-in, belt, wrap)
V-neck, scoop neck, sweetheart neck (natural bust line)
Wrap dresses, fit-and-flare, belted dresses
Pencil skirt (for curves) or full skirt (with tuck-in)
Medium fit flatters best
Draping fabrics (jersey, silk, cotton blend)
Single-breasted, short jackets, belted coats
dontList: Overly boxy oversized fits (hides your lines)
Long outerwear that hides the waist
Thick knits or chunky fabrics (adds bulk)

male.desc: You have balanced shoulders and hips with a well-defined waist. Highlighting your lines looks amazing.
doList: Fits that accentuate the waistline (tuck-in, belt)
Medium fit flatters best
Solid colors with accent items
Fabrics that drape naturally on the body
Both V-neck and crew neck work well
Slim-fit shirts, high-waist trousers
Single-breasted jacket (waist suppression)
Medium belt (contrasting color for accent)
dontList: Overly boxy oversized fits (hides your lines)

round.name: Round
round.subtitle: Belly-centered, rounded silhouette
round.colorSummary: Overall dark tones with vertical line emphasis

female.desc: You carry volume in the midsection. Creating vertical lines and drawing attention to neckline, arms, and legs creates a slimmer look.
doList: Deep V-neck, wide V, scoop neck, cowl neck to distribute attention
Empire waist, wrap tops, flowing draped tops
Empire waist, A-line, wrap, fit-and-flare dresses
3/4 sleeves for visually slimmer arms and detail focus
A-line, flare, knee-length+ skirts
High-waist straight or bootcut pants
Slim belt below bust or at high waist (tonal matching)
Single-breasted, open-front, structured jackets (hip length+)
Well-fitting bra is essential (foundation for silhouette)
dontList: Horizontal details or embellishments at waist

male.desc: You carry volume in the midsection. Vertical lines and dark center colors create a slimmer appearance.
doList: Dark colors on tops and mid layers for a slim center
Emphasize vertical lines (open jackets, cardigans)
Monochrome or similar tone outfits for a longer look
V-neck or deep crew neck to distribute attention
Structured jackets to organize the silhouette
Single-breasted, open jackets
Straight or slightly tapered pants

questions.question: Compare your shoulder and hip width:
How is your waistline?
options: My waist and hips are straight

questions.question: Where do you carry more volume — upper or lower body?
What concerns you most when choosing clothes?
options: Worried about top fit due to wide shoulders

questions.question: Which top fit suits you best?
